messages.content:
Customer ProfileCompany: Adobe Inc.
Department: Brand Concierge Product Management & Engineering
Industry: Software/SaaS - Digital Experience Platform
Use Case Category: Application Performance Monitoring, Product Analytics, Customer Experience
Primary Interest: APM, Custom Business Metrics, User Behavior AnalyticsProduct Context:
Adobe Brand Concierge is an enterprise SaaS platform enabling organizations to create, manage, and distribute brand assets through customizable brand portals.Scale & Environment:

850+ enterprise customer accounts (multi-tenant SaaS)
125,000+ daily active users
45M+ brand assets under management
8.5M+ daily API requests from integrated platforms
Multi-region AWS deployment (US-East, US-West, EU-West, APAC)
15-person product team, 60-person engineering organization
40+ technology partner integrations (Workfront, Marketo, Salesforce)
Pain PointsFragmented product usage visibility: Product team relies on disparate data sources (Google Analytics, Application Insights, Snowflake, Salesforce) to understand customer behavior. Answering questions like "Which customers use the AI-powered asset tagging feature?" requires 2-3 days of manual data extraction and reconciliation across systems. This fragmentation prevents real-time product insights and slows iteration velocity, with critical roadmap decisions lacking quantitative foundation.Disconnected user experience and technical performance: When customers report platform slowness, the team cannot correlate complaints with actual performance data. Engineering monitors backend latency but cannot connect this to front-end experience metrics like page load times or workflow completion rates. The product team cannot answer: "Are EU customers experiencing worse performance?" or "Which accounts are hitting rate limits and potentially churning?"No early warning system for customer health: Customer Success identifies at-risk accounts only when obvious signals appear (support spikes, executive escalations)—often 30-60 days before renewal. The team cannot proactively detect declining login frequency, abandoned workflows, or decreasing API volumes indicating waning engagement. This costs an estimated $8-12M annually in preventable churn.Slow feedback loops on product releases: Understanding new feature impact requires waiting 4-6 weeks for monthly metrics reviews. By the time the team learns a feature has 8% adoption (vs 25% target), they're already committed to the next release cycle. The inability to measure feature performance within days of launch prevents rapid iteration.Multi-tenant performance isolation challenges: "Noisy neighbor" problems where one customer's heavy usage degrades response times for others require manual log analysis to identify. Engineers lack tools to quickly determine if slowness is global, tenant-specific, or caused by a customer's own inefficient API integration.Key Metrics & Data SourcesUser Engagement & Behavior Metrics (via Elastic APM RUM Agent)Collection Method: Elastic APM RUM JavaScript agent in Brand Concierge web application, capturing front-end interactions and custom business events.
Active User & Session Metrics:

Field names: user.id, user.organization.id, user.organization.tier, session.duration.ms, session.page_views, session.outcome
Description: DAU/MAU, session quality, engagement depth by customer tier and region
Breakdown: By customer tier, industry, geographic region, user role



Feature Usage Tracking:

Field names: feature.name, feature.action, feature.completion_status, feature.time_to_complete_ms
Description: Granular tracking of feature adoption (template builder, AI tagging, approval workflows)
Analysis: Funnel conversion rates, feature stickiness (DAU/MAU), drop-off points in workflows


Application Performance Metrics (via Elastic APM Agents)Collection Method: APM agents across the stack: Java (Spring Boot APIs), Node.js (rendering layer), Python (ML microservices), .NET (asset processing).
API & Transaction Performance:

Field names: transaction.name, transaction.duration.us, transaction.result, service.name, trace.id
Description: End-to-end API latency, database query performance, external service dependencies
SLA targets: P95 latency <500ms for reads, <2s for writes



Multi-Tenant Performance Isolation:

Field names: tenant.id, tenant.tier, transaction.duration.us, tenant.resource.cpu_seconds, tenant.resource.db_queries
Description: Per-tenant performance tracking, resource consumption attribution, noisy neighbor detection
Source: APM custom context injection, CloudWatch metrics with tenant tagging


Business & Product Metrics (via Custom Instrumentation)Collection Method: Application events via Logstash HTTP input, enriched with customer metadata from tenant database.
Asset & Content Metrics:

Field names: asset.action, asset.type, asset.size_bytes, portal.visitor_count, template.customization_rate
Description: Asset lifecycle events, portal engagement, template usage patterns
Business insight: Download/share rates indicate content value



Collaboration & Workflow Metrics:

Field names: workflow.type, workflow.duration_hours, workflow.status, workflow.iteration_count
Description: Asset review/approval cycle times, workflow completion/abandonment
Efficiency metric: Long cycles indicate process friction


API & Integration Metrics (via APM + API Gateway Logs)Collection Method: AWS API Gateway logs via Kinesis Firehose, APM instrumentation on API services.
Partner Ecosystem Health:

Field names: api.consumer.type, api.endpoint, api.success_rate_pct, webhook.delivery_status
Description: API usage by partner (Workfront, Marketo, Salesforce), error rates, webhook reliability
SLA tracking: Enterprise partners guaranteed 99.9% availability


Customer Success Metrics (via Elasticsearch Transforms + ML)Collection Method: Calculated within Elasticsearch using transforms, ML anomaly detection, enriched with Salesforce data.
Customer Health Scoring:

Field names: customer.health_score, customer.engagement_trend, customer.churn_risk_probability, customer.days_to_renewal
Description: Composite health score (0-100) from 30+ usage signals, ML-calculated churn risk
Alert thresholds: <40 = critical risk, 40-60 = at risk, 60-80 = healthy, >80 = thriving


Infrastructure Metrics (via Metricbeat + CloudWatch)Collection Method: Metricbeat on ECS containers, CloudWatch streaming via Lambda.
Resource Consumption & Cost:

Field names: tenant.cost.infrastructure_monthly, tenant.resource.storage_gb, ecs.task.cpu.pct, cdn.cache_status
Description: Per-tenant cost attribution, infrastructure health, CDN performance
Use case: Fair use enforcement, capacity planning


Use Cases1. Real-Time Feature Adoption Analysis & Funnel OptimizationObjective: Track granular feature usage patterns and workflow conversion rates to enable data-driven roadmap prioritization, reducing feedback loops from 4-6 weeks to 3-7 days.Implementation:

Instrument Brand Concierge with custom APM transactions for each major feature
Use APM custom spans to track multi-step workflow completion and drop-off points
Create Elasticsearch transforms for feature adoption rollups: DAU/MAU per feature, feature combinations, time-to-first-use
Build Kibana Lens funnels showing conversion rates through workflows
Configure ML anomaly detection for unusual feature usage patterns (viral adoption or sudden drops)
Key Metrics:

feature.adoption_rate = (users who used feature in last 30 days / total active users) * 100
feature.stickiness = DAU/MAU ratio per feature
funnel.conversion_rate for multi-step workflows
feature.time_to_first_use_days = days between account creation and feature adoption
Output/Benefits:
"Template builder has 78% adoption among Enterprise but only 23% among Professional tier - investigation reveals onboarding doesn't emphasize templates. Added template showcase to Pro welcome flow, adoption increased to 51% in 2 weeks"2. Proactive Customer Health Monitoring & Churn PreventionObjective: Identify at-risk customers 60-90 days before renewal using data-driven health scoring, reducing annual churn from 12% to <8%.Implementation:

Create Elasticsearch transform aggregating 30+ usage signals: login frequency, asset uploads, API volume, support tickets, feature adoption breadth
Implement weighted health scoring algorithm with ML baseline deviation detection
Configure trend analysis (7-day vs 30-day moving averages) to detect gradual disengagement
Build multi-tier alerting: Critical (<40), At-risk (40-60), Declining (score dropped >15 points in 2 weeks)
Generate automated root cause playbooks when health scores drop
Key Metrics:

customer.health_score (0-100 composite) calculated from weighted usage signals
customer.engagement_trend: increasing/stable/declining/critical
customer.churn_risk_probability from ML classification model
customer.feature_adoption_breadth = distinct features used in last 30 days
Output/Benefits:
"Customer 'Nike EMEA' health score dropped from 78 to 52 over 14 days. Root cause: 85% decline in asset uploads + API calls stopped. Investigation revealed broken Workfront integration after platform upgrade. CS team proactively fixed integration, avoided $450K contract churn"3. Multi-Tenant Performance Monitoring & SLA ManagementObjective: Ensure fair resource allocation across 850+ tenants, detect "noisy neighbor" situations, and guarantee SLA compliance (P95 API latency <500ms, 99.9% uptime).Implementation:

Deploy APM agents with custom tenant ID propagation through all transactions, spans, and errors
Instrument AWS infrastructure with tenant-aware CloudWatch metrics (ECS, RDS, S3, CloudFront)
Create per-tenant SLA tracking: calculate hourly P50/P95/P99 latency, error rates, availability percentage
Implement real-time "noisy neighbor" detection using ML anomaly detection for resource usage
Configure dynamic rate limiting when tenants exceed fair-use thresholds
Key Metrics:

tenant.sla.availability_pct = (successful requests / total requests) * 100 per tenant
tenant.resource.cpu_seconds, tenant.resource.db_queries, tenant.resource.storage_gb
tenant.noisy_neighbor_score = ML anomaly score for resource consumption
transaction.duration.us aggregated by tenant for P95/P99 calculations
Output/Benefits:
"Customer 'Adobe Internal IT' reports slowness at 2:14pm. Elastic shows P95 latency spiked from 380ms to 1,850ms. Co-located tenant running massive batch upload saturating PostgreSQL connection pool. Moved tenant to isolated replica, restored performance in 8 minutes"4. A/B Testing & Product Experimentation PlatformObjective: Enable rapid experimentation by measuring feature variant performance against business KPIs, making go/no-go decisions within days of launch.Implementation:

Integrate feature flag service with Elastic APM: propagate experiment.id, experiment.variant through transaction context
Instrument conversion events as custom APM transactions tagged with experiment variants
Create Elasticsearch transforms calculating conversion rates, statistical significance, confidence intervals per variant
Build experiment dashboard template showing primary/secondary/counter-metrics side-by-side
Configure automated winner detection when variant shows significant improvement (p<0.05, min 1,000 users)
Key Metrics:

experiment.conversion_rate per variant for primary success metric
experiment.statistical_significance = p-value from proportion test
experiment.uplift_pct = ((variant_metric - control_metric) / control_metric) * 100
transaction.duration.us by variant to detect performance regressions
Output/Benefits:
"Experiment #247: New template wizard vs existing flow. After 5 days with 2,400 users, variant shows 34% higher completion rate (p<0.001). Shipped to 100%, resulting in 6,500 additional templates/month"5. API Partner Ecosystem Health MonitoringObjective: Ensure third-party integrations operate reliably, maintain partner satisfaction, and identify issues before they escalate to partner churn.Implementation:

Segment API telemetry by integration partner: enrich API transactions with api.consumer.type, api.consumer.account_id
Create partner-specific dashboards showing request volumes, latency, error rates, rate limit consumption
Build webhook delivery monitoring tracking webhook.delivery_status, retry counts, failure reasons
Configure integration health scoring: API success rate + latency + webhook reliability + support tickets
Implement proactive partner outreach when integration health drops below 75
Key Metrics:

api.success_rate_pct = (2xx responses / total requests) * 100 per partner
webhook.delivery_success_rate per partner integration
partner.integration_health_score (0-100) from composite reliability metrics
partner.api_version tracking deprecated version usage
Output/Benefits:
"Workfront integration showing 15% error rate spike at 3:42am daily. Investigation reveals Workfront batch job uses expired auth tokens. Contacted Workfront engineering, resolved token refresh issue, error rate dropped to <1%"Data Model & Index StrategyRecommended Index Patternsapm-*: Standard Elastic APM indices (transactions, spans, errors, metrics)

ILM: 7 days hot, 30 days warm, 90 days cold
rum-*: Real User Monitoring from browser/mobile

ILM: 7 days hot, 60 days warm
business-events-{YYYY.MM}: Custom business events (assets, features, workflows)

Monthly rollover, ILM: 90 days hot, 12 months warm, 24 months cold
customer-health-rollup: Transform-generated customer aggregation (updated daily)

Single index, no rollover, maintains current state
api-logs-{YYYY.MM.dd}: API Gateway access logs

Daily rollover, ILM: 30 days hot, 90 days warm, 12 months cold
Critical Field Mappingsuser.organization.id: keyword - Customer tenant identifier
user.organization.tier: keyword - starter/professional/enterprise
user.organization.arr: scaled_float - Annual Recurring Revenue

feature.name: keyword - Feature identifier
feature.action: keyword - view/create/edit/delete/share
feature.completion_status: keyword - started/in_progress/completed/abandoned

transaction.name: keyword - API endpoint or operation
transaction.duration.us: long - Latency in microseconds
trace.id: keyword - Distributed trace ID

tenant.id: keyword - Tenant identifier for multi-tenant tracking
tenant.cost.infrastructure_monthly: scaled_float - Allocated AWS costs

customer.health_score: scaled_float - Composite score 0-100
customer.churn_risk_probability: float - ML-calculated (0.0-1.0)

api.consumer.type: keyword - workfront/marketo/salesforce/custom
api.endpoint: keyword - API path
http.response.status_code: integer

transaction.experience.lcp: float - Largest Contentful Paint (ms)
transaction.experience.fid: float - First Input Delay (ms)Implementation Timeline

Excellent! I've captured a comprehensive profile for Adobe Brand Concierge:

✅ **Company:** Adobe Inc. - Brand Concierge Product Management & Engineering
✅ **Industry:** Software/SaaS - Digital Experience Platform
✅ **Scale:** 850+ enterprise accounts, 125K+ DAU, 8.5M+ daily API requests, multi-region AWS
✅ **Pain Points:** 5 identified (fragmented visibility, disconnected performance data, no early warning system, slow feedback loops, multi-tenant isolation challenges)
✅ **Use Cases:** 5 detailed use cases (feature adoption analysis, customer health monitoring, multi-tenant performance, A/B testing, API partner monitoring)
✅ **Metrics:** 13+ KPIs captured (health scores, adoption rates, SLA metrics, experiment metrics, API success rates)

Your demo is **ready to generate**! Type **'generate'** to create your Elastic Agent Builder demo configuration.

💡 Don't have all the details? Just type **'skip'** and I'll use reasonable defaults.

pain_points: Multi-tenant performance isolation challenges - noisy neighbor problems require manual log analysis to identify
Slow feedback loops on product releases - 4-6 weeks to understand feature impact prevents rapid iteration
Fragmented product usage visibility across disparate data sources (Google Analytics, Application Insights, Snowflake, Salesforce) requiring 2-3 days of manual data extraction
Disconnected user experience and technical performance - cannot correlate customer complaints with actual performance data
No early warning system for customer health - at-risk accounts identified only 30-60 days before renewal, costing $8-12M annually in preventable churn
use_cases: API Partner Ecosystem Health Monitoring - ensure third-party integrations operate reliably, maintain partner satisfaction, identify issues before they escalate to partner churn

Multi-Tenant Performance Monitoring & SLA Management - ensure fair resource allocation across 850+ tenants, detect noisy neighbor situations, guarantee SLA compliance (P95 API latency <500ms, 99.9% uptime)
Proactive Customer Health Monitoring & Churn Prevention - identify at-risk customers 60-90 days before renewal using data-driven health scoring, reducing annual churn from 12% to <8%
A/B Testing & Product Experimentation Platform - enable rapid experimentation by measuring feature variant performance against business KPIs, making go/no-go decisions within days of launch
Real-Time Feature Adoption Analysis & Funnel Optimization - track granular feature usage patterns and workflow conversion rates, reducing feedback loops from 4-6 weeks to 3-7 days
context.scale: 850+ enterprise customer accounts, 125,000+ daily active users, 45M+ brand assets, 8.5M+ daily API requests, multi-region AWS deployment (US-East, US-West, EU-West, APAC), 15-person product team, 60-person engineering organization, 40+ technology partner integrations
metrics: customer.health_score - 0-100 composite score from weighted usage signals
tenant.sla.availability_pct - successful requests / total requests * 100 per tenant
feature.adoption_rate - users who used feature in last 30 days / total active users * 100
api.success_rate_pct - 2xx responses / total requests * 100 per partner
experiment.uplift_pct - variant vs control performance improvement
